summary: Drew DeVault announces his planned move from the United States to the Netherlands, citing deep dissonance with American values, particularly around capitalism and its incompatibility with FOSS culture. He acknowledges the move is a selfish choice, opting to live somewhere that already aligns with his values rather than staying to fight for change. He chose the Netherlands for its visa accessibility, high quality of life, strong EU FOSS ecosystem, and five-year path to citizenship. He also announces that SourceHut will be relocating its incorporated entity and infrastructure to the Netherlands.
key_points: The FOSS community in Europe is much stronger than in the US, where capitalism as 'secular religion' conflicts with FOSS values
DeVault frames leaving the US as a selfish but deliberate choice, refusing to spend his one life on politics while acknowledging his privilege in being able to leave
The Netherlands was chosen for practical reasons: visa accessibility, EU citizenship path, high quality of life, and existing European social network
SourceHut as a business entity and its infrastructure will also relocate to the Netherlands
He does not hold Americans accountable for their country's failures, recognizing how difficult emigration is for most people
money_quotes: In the United States, capitalism is the secular religion, and my values, in FOSS and otherwise, are incompatible with the American ethos.
Leaving the US is a selfish choice. I could stay here to get involved in solving these problems, but I chose to leave for a place which has already made much more progress on them.
Ultimately, this is the only life I'm gonna get, and I have decided not to spend it on politics.
Because I know how difficult it is to leave, for reasons unique to each person's own situation, I don't hold anyone who stays behind accountable for their country's cruelties.
It's quite clear that the FOSS community in Europe is much stronger than in America.
I'm under no illusions that the Netherlands is some kind of utopian place. I am well versed in the social, economic, and political issues they face.
By my reckoning, it's a hell of a lot better than the US, and will remain so for the foreseeable future.
key_insight: DeVault frames his emigration as an explicit rejection of American capitalism and its incompatibility with FOSS values, choosing to relocate both himself and SourceHut to a society more aligned with his principles rather than fighting a losing cultural battle.
